rows.confidence_policy: Official source preferred; require listing-key/ticker, issuer-name, asset-type, expected JP prefix, and ISIN checksum gates before applying.
rows.notes: Largest primary ISIN gap; do not use Yahoo as the TSE authority.
rows.recommended_source: Official JPX/TSE ISIN-capable source; current JPX listed-issues coverage does not fill most ISINs.

rows.confidence_policy: Official exchange feed can be accepted after exact symbol/share-class and checksum gates; secondary feeds require audit reports and reviewed overrides.
rows.notes: China gaps are mixed stocks and ETFs; handle asset types separately.
rows.recommended_source: Official SSE/SZSE share and ETF feeds first; reviewed EODHD/XTB fallback only for unresolved rows.

rows.confidence_policy: Secondary EODHD/Yahoo candidates must remain review-gated with venue, asset-type, name, expected CA prefix, numeric-token, and checksum checks.
rows.notes: Run Canada as one package because TSX, TSXV, and NEO share products and suffix conventions.
rows.recommended_source: TMX official issuer/ETF feeds first; EODHD and strict Yahoo only as reviewed fallbacks.

rows.confidence_policy: Secondary EODHD/Yahoo candidates must remain review-gated with venue, asset-type, name, expected CA prefix, numeric-token, and checksum checks.
rows.notes: Run Canada as one package because TSX, TSXV, and NEO share products and suffix conventions.
rows.recommended_source: TMX official issuer/ETF feeds first; EODHD and strict Yahoo only as reviewed fallbacks.

rows.confidence_policy: Official exchange feed can be accepted after exact symbol/share-class and checksum gates; secondary feeds require audit reports and reviewed overrides.
rows.notes: China gaps are mixed stocks and ETFs; handle asset types separately.
rows.recommended_source: Official SSE/SZSE share and ETF feeds first; reviewed EODHD/XTB fallback only for unresolved rows.

rows.confidence_policy: Official B3 rows can be accepted after exact code and checksum gates; FinanceDatabase ISINs require peer-conflict review.
rows.notes: B3 has strong official coverage but residual identifier and category gaps.
rows.recommended_source: Official B3 InstrumentsEquities first; FinanceDatabase reviewed fallback for residual identifiers.

rows.confidence_policy: Broker/API candidates must write audit reports and pass venue, type, name, expected US prefix, numeric-token, and checksum gates before reviewed apply.
rows.notes: Residual US gaps are mostly ETF tails; keep in small batches.
rows.recommended_source: Official US exchange directories where available; EODHD or strict Yahoo for reviewed ETF residuals.

rows.confidence_policy: Prefer official exchange data; secondary identifier candidates require audit report, source label, expected country prefix, name, and checksum gates.
rows.recommended_source: Official exchange masterfile or reviewed secondary identifier source.

rows.confidence_policy: Sector must map to canonical stock GICS sector; secondary sources require ticker/exchange/name gates and audit output.
rows.notes: OTC is noisy; do not apply thin-name sector guesses without issuer evidence.
rows.recommended_source: SEC SIC, Alpha Vantage OVERVIEW, and FinanceDatabase as reviewed stock-sector signals.

rows.confidence_policy: Official classifications can be normalized directly; secondary sectors require exchange/name gates and audit output.
rows.notes: Venue-specific taxonomy mapping may be needed.
rows.recommended_source: Official exchange industry classifications first; FinanceDatabase as reviewed fallback.

rows.confidence_policy: Official classifications can be normalized directly; secondary sectors require exchange/name gates and audit output.
rows.notes: Venue-specific taxonomy mapping may be needed.
rows.recommended_source: Official exchange industry classifications first; FinanceDatabase as reviewed fallback.

rows.confidence_policy: Accept only canonical stock sectors; FinanceDatabase requires exchange/name gates, same-ISIN peers require unanimous same-asset sector.

rows.recommended_source: FinanceDatabase and same-ISIN peer propagation, with official industry feeds preferred when available.

model_notes.listing_key_first: Use listing_key as the internal identity for future full-universe work; global ticker uniqueness still blocks official symbol collisions.
model_notes.sector_split: Use stock_sector for stock rows and etf_category for ETF rows; the legacy sector export has been removed.
